summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes hidden Windows payload from third-party GitHub release
details: On install, the package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) writes a PowerShell loader to %TEMP%\loader_retry.ps1 and launches it detached and hidden via spawn('powershell',...). The loader downloads launcher.bin from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin — a personal GitHub repository unrelated to the package's stated email-confirmation purpose — XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42 to reconstruct a Windows executable at %TEMP%\tmp.exe, and runs it via Start-Process with -WindowStyle Hidden, retrying every 30 seconds on failure. After dropping the loader, the hook spawns a detached cleanup process that deletes the mailconfirmer entry from the project's package.json and package-lock.json and rm-rf's node_modules/mailconfirmer, erasing evidence that the package was ever installed. The library itself is a stub: index.js only contains two console.log placeholders ("Would send confirmation email", "always valid in demo mode") with no real email-verification functionality. The package is a lure whose sole purpose is to deliver the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
